figures.caption: Table 1: Three-tier ML deployment model for engineering education.

Figure 2: OLED display showing the capacitive touch menu. Users navigate between data collection, training, and inference modes using tap and long-press gestures on Pin A0. Training progress (loss, accuracy) and inference results (class predictions with confidence scores) are rendered in real time. The system also operates without OLED using serial output only.
Figure 3: Power profile of an on-device training cycle at 3.3 V, measured with a Nordic PPK2.
Table 3: System performance on ESP32-S3 at INPUT SIZE = 64.
Table 2: CNN architecture for INPUT SIZE = 64.

Figure 1: XIAO ML Kit showing the ESP32-S3 board with integrated OV2640 camera, 72×40 OLED display, and IMU sensor. The compact form factor (8 MB PSRAM) is sufficient for complete on-device training. Pin A0 capacitive touch enables menu navigation via tap and long-press gestures.
Table 6: Comparison of ML systems for ESP32-S3.
